title: Architectural floorplan generation

china-office.note_html: Floorplan generated from chinaOffice.rrd (no existing drawing needed) — 3 levels auto-detected, sealed room loops, doors inferred from trajectory crossings. AI review + plan critique ran on all 3 levels, and the skill's default stylized renditions (drafted / cyanotype / presentation, one full-resolution image per level rather than a cramped combined sheet) all succeeded on the 2026-07-14 run below.

human-activity.reason: A floorplan needs a static structural scan of the building; the activity scenario is about moving people, not mapping the space.

summary_html: Multi-story architectural drawing sets (DXF with AIA layers + JPEG sheets) from lidar — live or from a .rrd recording — now wrapped as an agent-callable @skill: FloorplanSkillContainer.generate_floorplan() generates the drawings and returns measured attributes of the navigated space (levels + FFLs, wall/door/stair counts, glazed fraction, mezzanines/sunken areas, extents). Debug artifacts and 3D-model output are OFF by default for MCP callers and cleaned up automatically. Stylized AI renditions (drafted / cyanotype / presentation, via the OpenAI image model) are ON by default and attached to the skill's response as images the calling agent can actually see — not just file paths — so a touring agent can use them to answer layout questions.

skills.name: generate_floorplan(rrd_path?, duration?, explore?, …)
skills.desc_html: Generates the DXF + JPEG drawing set (optionally exploring first, or from a recording) and answers with measured attributes of the space: levels + FFLs, wall/door/stair counts, glazed fraction, extents. Optional save_3d_model for the sliceable .rrd.

figures.alt: Three cyanotype floor-plan sheets (LEVEL 1-3) of a real office building with sealed room-loop outlines: double-line walls, glazing, door swings, dimension strings, north arrows, scale bars, and a CHINA OFFICE title block
figures.caption_html: Best result to date: the script's own JPEG sheet from the real chinaOffice.rrd recording (67 MB, repo root), now with sealed room loops from the voxel model (dashed hairlines; 27 gap walls bridged across the three levels, all sealed at the smallest 0.3 m closing kernel). Three levels auto-detected (FFL +0.08 / +3.28 / +6.78 m), 60 door openings — and glazing separated from solid wall. Raw CAD file with the A-AREA loop layer: chinaoffice_loops.dxf. Stylized AI renditions of each level are in the China office test-data tab below.
figures.heading: Sample output — real office recording, three levels, sealed loops

figures.alt: Left: 3D voxel model of the china office with a translucent cut plane at 1.6 m and the slab's voxels highlighted red. Right: the horizontal section at that height showing crisp wall outlines.
figures.caption_html: What --viewer shows live in dimos-viewer, rendered statically: the analytic voxel model (SceneModel.voxel_occupancy(), 1.3M occupied voxels at 0.15 m) with the sweeping cut plane, and the wall section at that height. In the viewer the time slider is the cut plane — one horizontal section per z-step is logged on a cut_z timeline, so scrubbing sweeps the cut through the property; sealed room loops (cyan) and closure-inferred cells (orange) are overlaid. Regenerate: uv run python misc/skills_dashboard/scripts/gen_voxel_slicing_figure.py
figures.heading: Voxel model + cross-section slicing (--viewer)

how_it_works.title: Collect
how_it_works.html: Subscribes to /lidar + /global_map via LidarPointCloudClient (item 3) and tracks pose on /odom, optionally driving the robot (--explore / --spin). Alternatively loads an offline Rerun recording with --rrd building.rrd.

how_it_works.title: Detect floor levels
how_it_works.html: The robot's trajectory z-histogram reveals which floors it walked on. Stories must be ≥ 1.8 m apart; each level's occupancy band is capped 2.6 m above its floor, so roof points above the top story are excluded. Multi-story recordings become one sheet per level.

how_it_works.title: Rasterize & classify
how_it_works.html: Points are binned into occupancy grids per level. A cell is structural (wall) when lidar sees returns well above furniture height there; indoor is distinguished from outdoor by ceiling evidence — returns above head height. Trees, vehicles, and ground clutter drop out.

how_it_works.title: Vectorize
how_it_works.html: Finds the dominant wall angle, unrotates the grid, and extracts straight wall runs. Gaps < 0.35 m are bridged as noise; runs < 0.8 m are dropped; gaps of 0.55–1.25 m — and points where the robot's own trajectory crossed a wall run — become door openings with swing arcs. Envelope runs the lidar saw through are drawn as glazing (thin double line, layer A-GLAZ) rather than solid wall. Furniture keeps thin outlines on its own layer.

how_it_works.title: Seal room loops (voxel model)
how_it_works.html: The 3D VoxelOccupancy model (item 8) seals each connected area the robot drove through: progressive morphological closing finds the smallest kernel (0.3 → 2.5 m) at which the trajectory-seeded interior flood no longer leaks, bounded by the ceiling-evidence indoor mask so ground floors don't escape through entrances. The resulting closed loops land on layer A-AREA, and boundary spans not explained by any wall or door become bridging wall segments — doors stay open (the trajectory proved them passable). Disable with --no-close-loops.

how_it_works.title: AI review (optional)
how_it_works.html: A GPT-4o pass (--ai-review, on by default when OPENAI_API_KEY is set) inspects a rendered debug image of each level and can flip misclassified wall/furniture clusters.

how_it_works.title: Draft & export
how_it_works.html: Writes <out>.dxf (ANSI31 wall hatch, dimension strings in mm, layers A-WALL, A-WALL-PATT, A-DOOR, A-FURN, A-ANNO-DIMS, A-ANNO-SYMB, A-ANNO-TTLB) and a cyanotype JPEG sheet with title block and sheet numbers.

rows: 3 levels auto-detected; 299 interior wall runs, 60 doors, glazing separated from solid walls; 45 sealed room loops (A-AREA) with 27 gap walls bridged, every level sealed at the smallest 0.3 m kernel; AI review + plan critique + 9 stylized renditions (3 styles × 3 levels) all succeeded · full sheet / DXF (run 2026-07-14)
39 MB .model.rrd with 125 cut steps written in ~2 s and opened in dimos-viewer; unit-tested in item 8's suite (test_save_rerun_sliceable + 6 voxel/loop tests, all pass)
floorplan.dxf (repo root) — valid DXF, but sparse fragmented coverage from a short sim walk; motivated the level-detection + trajectory-door improvements used above
None yet — script-stage prototype; its collection layer is covered by item 3's 6 tests

examples.pre_html: # from a recording, with project title block python scripts/demo_lidar_floorplan.py --rrd chinaOffice.rrd --project "CHINA OFFICE" # same, plus the sliceable 3D model opened in dimos-viewer python scripts/demo_lidar_floorplan.py --rrd chinaOffice.rrd --viewer # live: explore for 2 minutes, then draft (no room-loop sealing) python scripts/demo_lidar_floorplan.py --duration 120 --explore --no-close-loops
examples.caption: outputs: <out>.dxf + <out>.jpg (+ <out>.model.rrd with --viewer)

next_step_html: Compose floorplan_skill_container into the agentic Go2 blueprints so the skill is live over MCP, and migrate the remaining rasterizing internals onto item 8's shared sections as they evolve.
dependencies: item 8 — SceneModel sections are the shared representation

test_descriptions.test_generate_floorplan_from_injected_mo: Full pipeline on a synthetic building: DXF+JPEG written, doorway found, no debug artifacts leak, summary reports the space attributes
test_descriptions.test_generate_floorplan_save_3d_model: save_3d_model=True writes the sliceable .model.rrd and the summary points at dimos-viewer
test_descriptions.test_skill_registers_with_schema: The @skill registers; its MCP schema documents every parameter and defaults debug/3D output OFF
test_descriptions.test_skill_fails_gracefully_on_missing_r: A bad recording path returns an error string instead of raising

tests_note_html: the skill-verification suite (dimos/skills/skill_verification) also runs the real generator on a recorded session — the DXF is built from the .rrd, no existing drawing needed — and writes the DXF, JPEG sheet and a DXF render to test_outputs/ for eyeball review, indexed by REVIEW.md. It's gated on data presence (needs a recording + ezdxf), so it runs locally on the sample recordings and no-ops in CI

manual_tests.name: test_generate_floorplan_from_recording
manual_tests.desc_html: Skill-verification: generate_floorplan() from a .rrd recording (chinaOffice.rrd) yields a DXF with wall geometry on A-WALL + a JPEG sheet; renders the DXF to a PNG for review
